zh.lifeSummary: 當William Gill於大約1528年出生在英國武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓時，他的父親William Gill-1歲，母親Margerie Wheeler-3歲。他於1553年9月25日與Margerie Wheeler在英國武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1604年在英國斯塔福爾金斯文福德享年77歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾金斯文福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Gill于大约1528年出生在英国武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓时，他的父亲William Gill-1岁，母亲Margerie Wheeler-3岁。他于1553年9月25日与Margerie Wheeler在英国武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1604年在英国斯塔福爾金斯文福德享年77岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾金斯文福德。

nameMeanings.name: Gill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Avtar, Amritpal, Darshan, Hardip, Nirmal, Sohan, Ajit, Balwinder, Charan, Jasvir, Kewal, Navdeep.

English and Scottish: in northern England and Scotland sometimes from Middle English Gille, Old Norse Gilli, which is of Irish (Gaelic) origin (see below), and pronounced with a hard g. As a personal name it is not found after c. 1200.

English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English gille ‘deep glen, ravine’ (Old Norse (Norwegian) gil), pronounced with a hard g. The term is found mainly in northwestern England, where Norwegian Vikings settled.

variants.name: Gilliam
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Gill(i)am, Gillem, Old French Guillaume, a Parisian form of Will(i)am (see William ).

variants.name: Gills
meanings: English: variant of Gill , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Alternatively, if pronounced with soft G-, possibly a variant of Giles .

variants.name: Gilson
meanings: English: ‘son of Gill’, from a Middle English pet form of either the female name Gillian (Latin Juliana), or of the male names Gilliam, a variant of William, and Giles. + -son. See Gill .
English: patronymic meaning ‘son of Giles’, from the personal name Giles + -son.
Irish (Down): adopted for Mac Giolla Seanáin, see Gilsenan .

variants.name: Guile
meanings: English (Sussex): altered form of Gyle, probably a variant of Gill .

variants.name: Gillan
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Giolláin, from a diminutive of giolla ‘lad, servant, devotee’. Compare Gill .

variants.name: Gilli
meanings: Swiss German and South German: from a pet form of the personal name   Gill   ‘Giles’.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Gillio, a vernacular derivative of Aegilius, a later form of Latin Aegidius (see Giles 1).
French (southern): of Italian origin (see 2 above) or a variant of   Gilles  .

variants.name: Gell
meanings: English (northern): from Middle English Gelle, a pet form of the personal name Gerard. Gerard in Middle English was indistinguishable from Gerald, of which Gelle was a syncopated form.

English: occasionally, a variant of Gill , from a pet form of the personal name Jelion or Gillian. These are Middle English versions of Julian, found mainly as a female name, occasionally as a male name.
Manx: variant of Gill , a shortened form of Gaelic Mac Gille, meaning ‘son of the lad (i.e. servant)’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Gill was born about 1528, in Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England, his father, William Gill, was -1 and his mother, Margerie Wheeler, was -3. He married Margerie Wheeler on 25 September 1553, in Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1604, in Kingswinford, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Kingswinford, Staffordshire, England.

Il est décédé en 1604, à Kingswinford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Kingswinford, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: William Gillが約1528年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベルブロウトンで生まれた時，父William Gillは-1歳，母Margerie Wheelerは-3歳であった。1553年9月25日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベルブロウトンでMargerie Wheelerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1604年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアキングスウィンフォードで77歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアキングスウィンフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Gill(이)가 대략 1528년 영국 워스스터실르 벨브루그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Gill(은)는 -1세였고, 그의 어머니 Margerie Wheeler(은)는 -3세였다. 그는 1553년 9월 25일 영국 워스스터실르 벨브루그h턴에서 Margerie Wheeler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1604년 영국 스태퍼드실르 킨그스윈퍼드에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 킨그스윈퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Gill nasceu aproximadamente 1528, em Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Gill, tinha -1 anos e sua mãe, Margerie Wheeler, tinha -3 anos. Ele casou-se com Margerie Wheeler em 25 de setembro de 1553, em Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1604, em Kingswinford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em Kingswinford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Gill родился около 1528 г. в местности белброугтон, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Gill было -1 год, а матери, которую звали Margerie Wheeler, -3. Он женился на женщине по имени Margerie Wheeler 25 сентября 1553 г. в местности белброугтон, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1604 г. в местности кингсвинфорд, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности кингсвинфорд, стаффордшир, Англия.
